title: wip: brelt non-record scaling package on 2xh100
author: guilhhotina

data.description: Recurrent global latent core reuses shared depth to mix span latents instead of a fully unique deep stack.
Shared recurrent depth / reused block structure across iterations.
Learned byte-level segmentation into patches, patch commit into latent span states, and bridge back to local byte space for prediction.

novel_contributions: Non-record scaling run of brelt on 2x H100 for 20 minutes
Archived exact code snapshot, logs, and submission package for a serious scaling experiment
Demonstrated that recurrent global latent mixing remains trainable at large H100 scale
Identified segmentation fragmentation as the main scaling pathology, with patch counts rising toward ~190 and latent rate collapsing
Included same-budget baseline comparison against the original challenge model
Used int8 plus zlib export with rotation of weight matrices before quantization
